title: cistus salviifolius var. microphyllus
intro: Cistus salviifolius var. microphyllus, also known as a dwarf or small-leaf rockrose, is a shrubby perennial plant in the family Cistaceae, characterized by its small, oval-shaped leaves and pink to purple flowers. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Cistus salviifolius var. microphyllus include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and soothe respiratory issues such as coughs and bronchitis. The plant can be used to make herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
